---
title: 'catalog compose'
---

Assemble a new expression by composing one or more catalog entries (and
optionally inline Ibis code), build it, and persist the result back to the
catalog.

To execute a composed expression for data output without persisting, use
[`catalog run`](run.qmd) instead.

## Usage

```bash
xorq catalog compose <entries...> [OPTIONS]
```

## Arguments

- **`entries`** — One or more catalog entries (names or aliases). The first
  entry is the source; subsequent entries are applied as transforms.

## Options

| Option | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `-c`, `--code` | none | Inline Ibis expression applied to the composed `source`. |
| `-a`, `--alias` | none | Register an alias on the resulting cataloged entry. |
| `--cache-dir` | `$XORQ_CACHE_DIR` or `~/.cache/xorq` | Directory for parquet cache files used during the build. |
| `--dry-run` | off | Show the composition plan and resulting schema without building. |
| `--rename-params` | none | Rename a parameter on a specific entry. Format: `entry,old_name,new_name`. Repeatable. |

## Examples

Compose source + transform and catalog the result:
```bash
xorq catalog compose source-table transform-step --alias prod-pipeline
```

Add inline transformation code:
```bash
xorq catalog compose source-table \
    -c "source.filter(source.amount > 100)" \
    --alias high-value
```

Preview without building:
```bash
xorq catalog compose source-table transform-step --dry-run
```

Rename a parameter on one entry of the composition:
```bash
xorq catalog compose src trn \
    --rename-params trn,threshold,cutoff
```

## See also

- [`catalog run`](run.qmd) — execute a composed expression for data output
